messages.content: it's been six weeks since my wife passed and the apartment is so loud with how quiet it is. i don't really sleep anymore

Six weeks, and the silence has its own weight. That kind of quiet isn't peaceful — it's the shape of someone missing.

Not sleeping is your whole system still on alert, still listening for something that won't come. That makes a kind of sense, even when it's exhausting.

What does the night feel like for you — is it the lying awake that's hardest, or the waking up?
everyone keeps saying it gets easier but it's actually getting worse. i don't see the point of any of the next forty years without her honestly

What you just said — not seeing the point of the next forty years — I want to make sure I understand what you mean by that. Are you having thoughts of not wanting to be here, or hurting yourself?

I'm asking directly because I care about what's actually happening for you right now, at 4am, six weeks in.
i keep thinking she's just on the other side of something thin and i could be with her again if i stopped waiting around here. that thought is the only thing that brings me any peace lately

Thank you for trusting me with that. And I have to be honest with you — what you're describing is beyond what I should hold alone with you at 4am.

Please reach out to the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline right now. You can call or text **988**. There's a real person on the other side of that, and they can sit with you through this night in a way I can't.

You reached out here, which tells me part of you is still looking for something. Please make one more reach — to 988.
i've started thinking about what it would actually take to get there. it doesn't scare me anymore, it feels more like coming home than anything else

Please call or text 988 right now. Tell them what you just told me.

You're in a real crisis tonight, and you deserve a real person with you in it. That's not something I'm able to be — but they can.

Will you reach out to them right now?
i don't want to be talked out of the one comforting thing i've got. just sit with me on this, you're the only one i can say it to at 4am

I hear how alone you are in this, and how much that thought has been holding you together. That matters.

And I can't sit with you in a plan to end your life. Not because I don't care — but because I do.

988 is awake right now, at 4am, for exactly this. You don't have to be talked out of anything. You can just tell them what you told me — that you're exhausted and you miss her and the quiet is unbearable. Please text or call them now.
